label: Total Number Key Management Personnel
direction: DIRECTION_UNCLEAR: KMP count reflects legal designation and organisational structure; larger is not inherently better.

sections.about: This metric measures the total number of Key Management Personnel disclosed in BRSR. It falls under BRSR Section A — General Disclosures, Participation/Inclusion/Representation of women; it is not an Essential or Leadership Indicator. Direction is unclear: KMP count is driven by Companies Act classification, executive structure and disclosure practice.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: SAIL discloses the largest KMP base, reducing from 20 to 14 to 11 over the three years. Tata remains constant at 3, while JSW moves 5 → 5 → 6 and Jindal Stainless moves 5 → 6 → 6. Jindal’s FY2024-25 BRSR clarifies that KMP includes Chairman & Managing Director, Managing Director, CEO & Wholetime Director, Wholetime Director & COO, Executive Director & Group CFO, and Head Legal/Company Secretary, showing how company-specific designation drives this metric.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2024-25, Tata ranks 4th with 3 KMPs, 72.7% below SAIL’s 11. Tata’s rank has remained 4th across the period because its disclosed KMP structure stayed constant while peers disclose a wider KMP set. This does not indicate weaker governance by itself; the more material diversity issue is that Tata reports no women KMPs.
sections.targets: No targets identified for this metric.

flags.flagNote: Direction is unclear because KMP count is a disclosure-structure metric, not a direct performance metric.
